titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Student Unrest at Yale Collection
titlestmt.author: compiled by staff of Manuscripts and Archives

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 30, 2018 at 08:33
finding aid revisiion description not supplied

did.unittitle: Student unrest at Yale collection
did.abstract: The materials consist of petitions, correspondence, photographs, notes, and clippings documenting various student riots and protests at Yale.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 41 (YRG 41), Material documenting Yale student life.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: While this collection as a whole is available for research, parts of it may be restricted due to law, university policy or fragility. Any restricted material will be noted as such.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Student Unrest at Yale Collection (RU 83). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials are aranged by accessions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The materials consist of petitions, correspondence, photographs, notes, and clippings documenting various student riots and protests at Yale.

did.unittitle: Materials documenting Yale student unrest

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Materials in Box 4, Folders 32-33 are restricted until January 1, 2036 and January 1, 2045 as established by Yale Corporation regulations.

did.unittitle: Conic Section Rebellion
Petitions, statements by the College, student apologies
Petitions, dismissals, statements by the College
Correspondence
Apologies from students of the class of 1829
Apologies from students of the class of 1830
Apologies from students of the class of 1831
Apology from one student from the class of 1832 and one unknown
Album commemorating the "Great Rebellion" with autograph sentiments by Yale students. Owned by George Edward Hand (Y. 1829)
Conic Rebellion
Statements and drafts of statements
Petitions and dismissals
Correspondence
Petition for "full publicity as to the purpose and character of the new addition [building] to the campus"
Reports and statements of witnesses
Report of the "ice cream" riot of 1952 and statements of the Snowball Riot
ALS [copy] from A. Whitney Griswold to students
Correspondence, statements, articles, clippings
Correspondence, statements, clippings from C. H. Sanford files
Nuts and Cranks
Photographs
Clippings

did.unitdate: 1959 March 13 - 16, 1957 March 17 - 20, 1959 March 22 March 31, 1959 April 1 - 1960 November 2

did.unittitle: Undated and duplicates
Black arm bands, "14 Mar"
Film reel (16 mm color): 1 video tape--dub of riot tape made by Anthony Moore (Y. 1959), 1 used cassette of audio recording
Riot of 1918
Legal papers
Correspondence
Notes and clippings
Clippings
Wright Hall Incident
Correspondence, statements, petitions [not restricted]
Calhoun College incident Executive Committee papers

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Materials in Box 4, Folders 32-33 are restricted until January 1, 2036 as established by Yale Corporation regulations.

did.unittitle: Wright Hall incident Executive Committee papers

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Materials in Box 4, Folder 33 are restricted until January 1, 2045 as established by Yale Corporation regulations.

did.unittitle: Pledge not to haze members of the class of 1871. Signed by 40 members of the class of 1870
Petition to Anson Phelps Stokes signed by 190 members of the class of 1921 asking for 2 tickets instead of 1 for each graduating student
Anonymous petition to have Thomas A. Marshall and not John M. Clayton nor John D. Eccles give the Valedictory Oration

unittitle.emph: Petition for Samuel S. Baldwin Y. 1801

did.unittitle: Petition of the Sophomore Class to the Authority of Yale College to re-examine the punishment for his offence
An Indian of the Mohegan tribe--holograph petition to the students of Yale College
Yale Women's Center, "Why the Symbols?"
"We are Yale Seniors Against the War"
Protest of the class of 1830 to the censure of four classmates

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

did.unittitle: Protest of the class of 1830 to the censure of four classmates
Flyers documenting student life at Yale

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

did.unittitle: Flyers documenting student life at Yale
Open letter to Staughton Lynd

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

did.unittitle: Open letter from Daniel V. Manningham to Professor Staughton Lynd
Letter from J.D. Wilcox to Dr. Phelps Humphreys about the Medical School riot

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

did.unittitle: Letter from J.D. Wilcox to Dr. Phelps Humphreys about the Medical School riot
Report on events at the School of Organization and Management

accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

did.unittitle: "A Note Describing Recent Events at the Yale School of Organization and Management," by Matthew C. Rogers '89, John C.A. Stevenson '89, Peter S. Szabo '90
"MAYDAY," a film made by Jericho Pictures for Yale Reports

accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
